reason: This role presents a high burnout risk due to a substantial physical toll from lifting anesthetized patients and the chronic stress of rapid operating room turnovers. Technicians face significant emotional intensity through exposure to acute trauma and end-of-life care, contributing to reported emotional exhaustion rates as high as 48%. Furthermore, the 'invisible' nature of the high-stakes technical troubleshooting required to prevent catastrophic equipment failures creates a constant, draining mental burden.
